name: Credit Advisors Foundation
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling organization offering debt management plans, bankruptcy counseling, and financial education since 1991. NFCC-certified counselors help consumers reduce debt payments up to 50%.
description_long:
Credit Advisors Foundation is a non-profit credit counseling organization that has been operating since 1991, with physical offices in Omaha, Nebraska; Scottsdale, Arizona; and Council Bluffs, Iowa. The organization is built on a mission to empower consumers to effectively manage their finances, create economic security, and ensure equality of access and opportunity. They serve as a free-help resource for individuals struggling with debt and financial management.

The organization offers comprehensive credit counseling and debt management services including debt management plans (DMPs) that consolidate multiple bills into one affordable payment, bankruptcy counseling for those considering Chapter 7 or 13 filing, housing and rental counseling for mortgage or rental payment issues, and extensive financial education classes and resources. They provide free confidential counseling as the foundation of their service model. Their counselors are certified personal finance counselors in consumer credit who function as client advocates rather than sales representatives.

Credit Advisors distinguishes itself through its commitment to quality assurance, multilingual support (Spanish, Korean, and Thai counselors available), and a transparent approach emphasizing dignity in the debt resolution process. The organization collects and analyzes performance data at all organizational levels and maintains ongoing dialogue with stakeholders including clients, creditors, regulators, and community partners. They also offer educational speakers and financial publications beyond direct counseling services.

As a legitimate non-profit, Credit Advisors Foundation provides genuinely free services without hidden fees or deceptive practices common in the debt relief industry. However, consumers should understand that debt management plans require creditor approval and work best for those with stable income who can commit to a multi-year repayment schedule. The organization's payment reduction claims (up to 50%) depend entirely on individual creditor negotiations and are not guaranteed.
pros: Certified non-profit providing genuinely free confidential counseling without sales commissions
Debt Management Plans consolidate multiple bills into single affordable monthly payment
Payment reductions up to 50% through creditor negotiations (on successful plans)
Multi-lingual counseling available in Spanish, Korean, and Thai
Operating since 1991 with established track record helping thousands of clients
Comprehensive services including bankruptcy counseling and housing counseling beyond debt management
Formal Quality Assurance Program measuring outcomes and performance across organization
cons: Debt Management Plans require creditor approval and do not work for all debt types (e.g., secured debt, student loans handled separately)
Payment reduction claims (up to 50%) are not guaranteed and depend on individual creditor negotiations
Limited geographic presence with only three physical office locations (Omaha, Scottsdale, Council Bluffs)
Multi-year commitment required for debt management plans, not suitable for those needing immediate debt elimination
Website lacks specific information about counselor credentials, NFCC certification status, or consumer complaint history
best_for: Consumers with multiple unsecured debts (credit cards, medical bills) seeking affordable consolidation without bankruptcy
Individuals considering bankruptcy who need counseling to explore alternatives first
Homeowners or renters struggling with housing payments needing specialized mortgage/rental guidance
Low-to-moderate income individuals seeking free financial education and budgeting assistance
diagnosis: Credit Advisors Foundation is ideal for consumers with multiple unsecured debts who have stable income and want to avoid bankruptcy through professional negotiation and consolidation. The primary caveat is that DMPs require creditor cooperation, work only on unsecured debts, and demand a multi-year commitment—making them unsuitable for those seeking immediate debt elimination or with unpredictable income.
services: Free credit counseling and budgeting services
Debt Management Plans with consolidated payments
Creditor negotiation and relief from collection calls
Guest speakers on credit responsibility and consumer finance
Client login portal for account management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 7525 E Camelback Rd, Suite 203, Scottsdale, AZ 85251
